State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814
Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement • • Statements of Economic Interest
(916) 322-5662          322-5660          322-5901          322-6441 • •          322-6444


April 10, 1985


Assemblyman Bill Jones
c/o District Office
1441 So. Mooney Blvd., Suite "D"
Visalia, CA 93277

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-85-074

Dear Assemblyman Jones:

You have written requesting advice regarding Chapter 1681,
Statutes of 1984 (AB 2992 Waters) as it applies to members of
city councils or boards of supervisors who act as members of the
board of a local Agency Formation Commission (LAFCO). Chapter
1681 amended Government Code Section 84308 in several respects.

As originally enacted Section 84308 applied only to
"quasi-judicial boards or commissions" and did not include
"legislative bodies such as city councils, county boards of
supervisors," etc. The Fair Political Practices Commission
considered the question of whether LAPCOs were covered by
Section 84308 (Curiel Opinion, 8 FPPC Opinions 1, copy enclosed)
and concluded that under the wording of Section 84308 they were
not covered, because their decisions were quasi-legislative in
nature rather than quasi-judicial. The Commission was
dissatisfied with the policy implications of the conclusion it
reached; however, it felt compelled to reach that conclusion
based upon the law.

The Commission introduced legislation (AB 2992 Waters) to
make a series of changes in the law, among them to alter the
coverage of Section 84308 so that it is no longer limited to
quasi-judicial boards or commissions and instead now includes

cr

'
r

Assemblyman bill Jones
April 10, 1985
Page 2

all agencies, with certain specified exceptions.1/ LAFCOs do
not fit within any of these exceptions and, hence, are covered
as a result of Chapter 1681, see enclosed copy of regulation
2 Cal. Adm. Code Section 18438.1.

As a result of being covered by the amended version of
Section 84308, a councilmember or supervisor who sits on a LAFCO
board may become subject to the restrictions contained in
Section 84308 regarding campaign contributions and campaign
contributors.

I trust that this letter has responded to your basic
question regarding Chapter 1681. However, the specific
application of these restrictions will depend upon the specific
facts of a given situation and it is very difficult to provide
further advice in the absence of concrete facts. If your
constituent desires further clarification, a further statement
of the facts would be of great assistance. If you have further
questions, please do not hesitate to call on us. I may be
reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosure

1/ Subsection (a)(3) of Section 84308 reads as follows:

(a) The definitions set forth in this subdivision
shall govern the interpretation of this section.

* * *

(3) "Agency" means an agency as defined in
Section 82003 except that it does not include the
courts or any agency in the judicial branch of
government, local governmental agencies whose members
are directly elected by the voters, the Legislature,
the Board of Equalization, or constitutional
officers. However, this section applies to any person
who is a member of an exempted agency but is acting as
a voting member of another agency.

SACRAMENTO ADDRESS
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA 95814
TELEPHONE: (916) 445-2931

DISTRICT OFFICE
1441 So. Mooney Blvd.
Suite "D"
Visalia, CA 93277
TELEPHONE: (209) 734-1182

DISTRICT OFFICE
State Building Suite 3688
2550 Mariposa Avenue
Fresno, CA 93721
TELEPHONE: (209) 237-0253

FROM: Porterville, Dinuba, Exeter,
Three Rivers and Springville
ENTERPRISE: 15483

Assembly
California Legislature

BILL JONES
ASSEMBLYMAN, THIRTY SECOND DISTRICT

VICE CHAIRMAN
ASSEMBLY LABOR & EMPLOYMENT COMMITTEE

VICE CHAIRMAN
ASSEMBLY RURAL CAUCUS

COMMITTEES:
Aging & Long Term Care
Agriculture
Labor & Employment
Public Investments,
Finance & Related
Insolvencies

Rural Caucus

Human Resources, Refugee
Resettlement, and
Immigration

REPLY TO
☐ Sacramento
☒ Visalia
☐ Fresno

March 20, 1985

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 "K" Street
Post Office Box 807
Sacramento, California 95804

Attention: Don Stanford, Chairman

Dear Mr. Stanford:

A constituent has asked for information pertaining to the
Government Code, Chapter 1681, as it pertains to members
of the City Council and/or Board of Supervisors acting as
members of the Board of LAFCO.

Will you please advise my office regarding this matter. Is
there a conflict in serving on the LAFCO Board if they have
received contributions for their campaigns from LAFCO?

Your attention to this matter is greatly appreciated.

Sincerely,

BILL JONES

BJ:bw
